items.x: Pick the menu bar pill style visually — Settings → Menu Bar now shows all three styles as live mockups; click one to use it.
The chip popover's Settings row shows and responds to ⌘,.
The chip opens on the first click — clicking it while another app was frontmost used to need a second click.
The last project row in Settings → Projects is no longer cut off.
Drag-and-drop project reordering in Settings → Projects — grab a row and drag; the order is used in the picker, popovers, and menus.
Settings from anywhere — a subtle ⚙ Settings button on the focus screen and a Settings row in the chip's popover.
"Reorder projects" and its up/down modal were removed from the focus screen — reordering lives in Settings now.
The Settings buttons outside the menu bar actually open the Settings window now.
Quick-switch buttons in the menu-bar popover — up to five color-coded emoji buttons under Stop/Switch jump straight to your most-worked projects.
"＋ New project…" in the chip popover (⌘N) and the menu bar's Switch menu — create a project without hunting through Settings.
Show chip toggle — hide the floating timer entirely; sessions keep running and the menu bar still shows progress.
New Settings sections with live previews — Menu Bar, Chip, App Icon, and Screen Glow each get their own sidebar page with a rendering of what they control.
Hide Dock icon — run DeepTime as a menu-bar-only app (Settings → App Icon).
Screen glow toggle — turn the under-menu-bar session glow on or off.
Picking an emoji while creating a project works again — the focus screen no longer eats the picker's clicks.
Editing a project's emoji and color together now saves both — previously only the last change stuck.
The beta-Telegram invite's ✕ now hides it everywhere, including Settings.
Fixed a crash when dismissing the beta-Telegram invite with its ✕ inside a popover.
